verses.text: This is the family history of Jesus Christ. He came from the family of David, who was from the family of Abraham.
Abraham was the father of Isaac, Isaac was the father of Jacob and Jacob was the father of Judas and his brothers.
Judas was the father of Perez and Zera and their mother was Tamar. Perez was the father of Esrom and Esrom was the father of Abam.
Aram was the father of Aminadab, Aminadab was the father of Naasson and Naasson was the father of Salmon.
Salmon was the father of Boaz and Boaz’s mother was Rahab. Boaz was the father of Obed and Obed’s mother was Ruth. Obed was the father of Jesse,
Jesse was the father of King David. David was the father of Solomon and Solomon’s mother was the wife of Uriah.
Solomon was the father of Reboam, Reboam was the father of Abia and Abia was the father of Asa.
Asa was the father of Jehoshaphat, Jehoshaphat was the father of Joram and Joram was the father of Uzziah.
Uzziah was the father of Joatham, Joatham was the father of Ahaz and Achaz was the father of Hezekiah.
Hezekiah was the father of Manasses, Manasses was the father of Amon and Amon was the father of Josiah.
Josiah was the father of Jechonias and his brothers. This was the time when the people of Judea were taken away to be slaves in the land of Babylon.
After the people of Judea were slaves in Babylon, Jechonias became the father of Salathiel. Salathiel was the father of Zerubbabel,
Zerubbabel was the father of Abiud, Abiud was the father of Eliakim and Eliakim was the father of Azor.
Azor was the father of Sadoc, Sadoc was the father of Achim and Achim was the father of Eliud.
Eliud was the father of Eleazar, Eleazar was the father of Matthan and Matthan was the father of Jacob.
Jacob was the father of Joseph and Joseph was Mary’s husband. Mary was the mother of Jesus who was named the Christ.

From Abraham to King David there were 14 generations. From King David to the time when the people of Judea became slaves in Babylon, there were 14 generations and from the time when they were slaves in Babylon to the time when Christ was born, there were 14 generations.

This is how the birth of Jesus Christ happened. His mother Mary was engaged to marry Joseph. But before they married, he learned that she was expecting a baby. (She was pregnant by the power of the Holy Spirit.)
Mary’s husband, Joseph, was a good man. He did not want to cause her public disgrace, so he planned to divorce her secretly.

But after Joseph thought about this, an angel from the Lord came to him in a dream. The angel said, Joseph, son of David, don’t be afraid to accept Mary to be your wife. The baby inside her is from the Holy Spirit.
She will have a Son and his name must be Jesus, because He will save his people from their sins.
All these things happened as the prophet had said:
The virgin will become pregnant, and she will have a son, and his name will be Emmanuel, Emmanuel means God is with us.
When Joseph woke up from his sleep, he did what the angel of the Lord had told him, and he married Mary.
He did not have sex with her. He waited until after the Child was born. Joseph named the Baby Jesus.

Jesus was born in the town of Bethlehem in the land of Judea. He was born at the time when Herod was king. After Jesus was born, there were some wise men who came to Jerusalem. They were from a land in the east.
The wise men was asking the people: Where is the Child who was born to be king of the Jews? We saw the star that shows he was born. We saw it rise in the sky in the east and have come to worship him
When they told King Herod about this, he and all the people in Jerusalem were upset and very worried.
King Herod called the chief priests and the teachers of the Laws. They had a meeting and King Herod asked them: Where will the Christ be born?
The chief priests and the teachers of the Laws said to Herod: The Christ will be born in the town of Bethlehem in the land of Judea. That is what the prophet wrote:
God said: Bethlehem, in the land of Judea, you a small place but a leader will come from you. He will be born in you and that ruler will lead Israel, my people.
Then King Herod called the wise privately. Herod asked the wise men when they had first seen the star.
Then King Herod said to the wise men: Go to Bethlehem and find the Child. And when you find Him, you must come back and tell me. I also want to go and worship Him.

After the king said this to the wise men, they went away. The star that they saw in the east went in front of them and they followed the star until it came to the place where the Child was and then the star stopped.
When the wise men saw the star there, they were very happy.

Then the wise men went into the house, and they saw the Child with his mother Mary. The wise bowed down and worshiped him. The wise men opened their bags, and they gave their gifts to the Child: They were gold, incense and myrrh.
God came to the wise men in a dream, and He warned them and said they must not go back to King Herod. That is why they went home on another road.

After the wise men went away, an angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and he said: Get up, take the Child and his mother and flee to Egypt. You must stay there until I tell you to come back home, because King Herod wants to kill the Child.
That night Joseph went away to Egypt. He took the Child and his mother with him.
They stayed in Egypt until King Herod died. This happened as the prophet had written: The Lord said, I, God, have called my son out of Egypt.

King Herod found out that the wise men from the east had deceived him. King Herod was very angry. He sent soldiers to kill all the boys in the town of Bethlehem who were 2 years old and younger than 2 years. They also killed the boys who lived near Bethlehem. King Herod told the soldiers to kill all the boys that were 2 years old and younger, because that was the time that the wise men told King Herod that they had seen the star.
All of this happened as the prophet Jeremiah wrote:
A sound was heard in Rama, crying and great sadness. Rachel cries for her children, and she cannot be comforted, because her children are dead.
After King Herod died, the angel of the Lord came to Joseph. He appeared to him in a dream in the land of Egypt. The angel said to Joseph:
Get up, take the Child and his mother and go back to the land of Israel, because the people who wanted to kill the Child have died.
Joseph took the Child and his mother, and he went back to Israel.

But Joseph heard that Archelaus was now king in Judea. Archelaus became king when his father Herod died. So, Joseph was afraid to go to Bethlehem. Then, after being warned in a dream, he went away to the area of Galilee
Then Joseph went to stay in the town of Nazareth. This happened because the prophet wrote, the people will say Jesus is a man from Nazareth.

3.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: In those days John the Baptist came, preaching in the desert of Judea.
He said: Turn away from your sins, because the kingdom of heaven is near.
John is the one the prophet Isaiah spoke about: A voice is calling out in the desert: Get the way ready for the Lord. Make a straight road for him.
John's clothes were made of camel hair, and he wore a leather belt around his waist. His food was locusts and wild honey.
People went out to him from Jerusalem, all Judea, and the whole area around the Jordan River.
They admitted their sins, and John baptized them in the Jordan River.
But when John saw many of the Pharisees and Sadducees coming to be baptized, he said to them: You family of snakes! Who warned you to run away from the coming anger of God?
Do the things that show you have really turned away from your sins.
Do not think you can say to yourselves: Abraham is our father. I tell you, God can make children for Abraham out of these stones.
The ax is already lying at the root of the trees. Every tree that does not produce good fruit will be cut down and thrown into the fire.

I baptize you with water to show you have turned from your sins. But someone is coming after me who is greater than I am. I am not good enough even to carry his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and with fire.
He has his shovel in his hand. He will clean off the threshing floor, gathering his wheat into the barn and burning up the useless husks with a fire that never goes out.
Then Jesus came from Galilee to the Jordan River to be baptized by John.
But John tried to stop him. He said: I need to be baptized by you. Why do you come to me?
Jesus answered: Let it be this way for now. We should do all that God asks. Then John agreed.
As soon as Jesus was baptized, he came up out of the water. At that moment heaven was opened, and he saw the Spirit of God coming down like a dove and resting on him.
And a voice from heaven said: This is my Son, whom I love. I am very pleased with him.
The Holy Spirit took Jesus into the desert so that the devil could test Him.
Jesus did not eat any food for 40 days and 40 nights. He was very hungry.
The devil came to Jesus and he tried to make Him do something wrong. The devil said to Jesus: If You are the Son of God, tell these stones to change into bread.
But Jesus answered and said, “It is written in the Old Testament, It is not just bread that keeps people alive. Their lives depend on what God says.’”
Then the devil took Jesus into Jerusalem, the Holy City, and put Jesus on a high place at the edge of the Temple area.

The devil said to Jesus: If You are the Son of God, then off, because it is written in the Old Testament: God will command his angels to help you, and their hands will catch you, so that you will not hit your foot on a rock.
Jesus said to the devil: It is also written in the Old Testament: You must not test the Lord your God.”
Then the devil took Jesus to a very high mountain. He showed Jesus all the lands of the world and all the wonderful things in them.
The devil said to Jesus: I will give all of this to You if You kneel before me and worship me.
Then Jesus said to the devil: Go away, Satan. In the Old Testament it is written: You must worship the Lord your God and serve only Him.
The devil went away from Jesus and angels came to help Jesus.
When they told Jesus that John the Baptist was in jail, Jesus went to Galilee.
Jesus did not stay in the town of Nazareth. He went to the town of Capernaum, near Lake Galilee. This town was next to Lake Galilee where the tribes of Zebulun and Naphtali had lived long before.
This happened because the prophet Isaiah had said:
Land of Zebulun and Naphtali, near the road to Lake Galilee, near the Jordan River, Galilee, land of people who are not Jews,
The people who live in spiritual darkness have seen a great light. The light has shined for those who live in the land that is as dark as a grave.
Then Jesus started to preach. Jesus said: You must turn to God and live as He wants, because God’s kingdom is now very near

Jesus walked on the beach of the Sea of Galilee. Jesus saw 2 brothers, Simon and Andrew. Simon also had another name. Simons other name was Peter. They were fishermen and they were busy putting their fishing nets into the lake.
Jesus said to Peter and Andrew, Come, follow me, and I will make you a different kind of fishermen. You will bring in people, not fish.
Simon and Andrew immediately stopped working with their fishing nets and they went with Jesus.

Jesus walked further and Jesus saw 2 other brothers, James and John. They were the sons of Zebedee. They were in the boat with their father and they were working with their fishing nets and mending them. Jesus called them to come with Him.
They immediately got out of their boat and went away from their father and went with Jesus.

Jesus went all over the land of Galilee, teaching people in the synagogues and preaching. Jesus told them the Good News that God in heaven rules over everyone. Jesus also shealed people who were sick.

The news about Jesus spread all over Syria, and people brought to him all those who were sick. They were suffering from different kinds of diseases and pain. Some had demons inside them, some suffered from seizures, and some who could not walk. Jesus healed them all.
There were many people who went with Jesus. They came from the Galilee, and from Decapolis, and from Jerusalem, and from Judaea, and from beyond Jordan.

5.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: When Jesus saw the crowds, he went up on a mountainside and sat down. His disciples came to him,
and he began to teach them. He said:
Blessed are those who know they are spiritually poor, because the kingdom of heaven belongs to them.
Blessed are those who are sad, because God will comfort them.
Blessed are the gentle, because they will receive the earth as their own.
Blessed are those who hunger and thirst to do what is right, because they will be satisfied.
Blessed are those who show mercy, because they will be shown mercy.
Blessed are those whose hearts are pure, because they will see God.
Blessed are those who make peace, because they will be called children of God.
Blessed are those who are treated badly for doing right, because the kingdom of heaven belongs to them.
Blessed are you when people insult you, treat you badly, and tell all kinds of lies about you because you follow me.
Be glad and full of joy, because a great reward is waiting for you in heaven. People treated the prophets who lived long ago in the same way.
You are the salt of the earth. But if salt loses its taste, how can it be made salty again? It is no longer good for anything, except to be thrown out and walked on by people.
You are the light of the world. A city built on a hill cannot be hidden.
People do not light a lamp and put it under a bowl. They put it on a stand, where it gives light to everyone in the house.
In the same way, let your light shine in front of others, so they can see the good things you do and praise your Father in heaven.
Do not think I have come to do away with the law of Moses or the writings of the prophets. I have not come to do away with them, but to fulfill them.
I tell you the truth, until heaven and earth disappear, not even the smallest letter or mark will disappear from the law until everything has come true.

So whoever breaks one of the least of these commands and teaches others to do the same will be called least in the kingdom of heaven. But whoever obeys these commands and teaches others to do the same will be called great in the kingdom of heaven.
I tell you, unless you do what is right better than the teachers of the law and the Pharisees do, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven.
You have heard that people long ago were told: Do not murder, and anyone who murders will be judged.

But I tell you, anyone who is angry with his brother will be judged. Anyone who insults his brother will have to answer to the court. And anyone who says, You fool! will be in danger of the fire of hell.
So if you are offering your gift at the altar and there remember that your brother has something against you,
leave your gift there in front of the altar. First go and make peace with your brother, and then come and offer your gift.

Make peace quickly with someone who is taking you to court. Do it while you are still on the way, or he may hand you over to the judge, the judge may hand you over to the officer, and you may be thrown into prison.
I tell you the truth, you will not get out until you have paid the last penny.
You have heard that it was said: Do not commit adultery.
But I tell you, anyone who looks at a woman and wants to sin with her has already committed adultery with her in his heart.
If your right eye causes you to sin, take it out and throw it away. It is better to lose one part of your body than for your whole body to be thrown into hell.
And if your right hand causes you to sin, cut it off and throw it away. It is better to lose one part of your body than for your whole body to go into hell.
It was also said: Anyone who divorces his wife must give her a written notice of divorce.
But I tell you, anyone who divorces his wife, except for sexual sin, causes her to commit adultery. And anyone who marries a divorced woman commits adultery.
Again, you have heard that people long ago were told: Do not break your promise, but keep the promises you make to the Lord.
But I tell you, do not make promises by swearing at all — not by heaven, because it is God's throne,
or by the earth, because it is the place where he rests his feet, or by Jerusalem, because it is the city of the great King.
And do not swear by your head, because you cannot make even one hair white or black.
Just let your yes be yes and your no be no. Anything more than this comes from the evil one.
You have heard that it was said: An eye for an eye, and a tooth for a tooth.
But I tell you, do not fight back against someone who is evil. If anyone slaps you on the right cheek, turn the other cheek to him too.
If anyone wants to sue you and take your shirt, let him have your coat as well.
If anyone forces you to go one mile, go with him two miles.
Give to the one who asks you, and do not turn away from the one who wants to borrow from you.
You have heard that it was said: Love your neighbor and hate your enemy.
But I tell you, love your enemies. Bless those who curse you. Do good to those who hate you. And pray for those who treat you badly and hurt you.
Then you will be children of your Father in heaven. He makes his sun rise on the evil and the good, and he sends rain on those who do right and those who do wrong.
If you love only those who love you, what reward will you get? Even the tax collectors do that.
And if you are kind only to your friends, what are you doing more than others? Even people who do not know God do that.
So be perfect, just as your Father in heaven is perfect.

6.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Be careful not to do your good deeds in front of others just to be seen by them. If you do, you will get no reward from your Father in heaven.

So when you give to the poor, do not announce it with trumpets, as the pretenders do in the synagogues and on the streets, so that others will praise them. I tell you the truth, they already have their full reward.
But when you give to the poor, do not let your left hand know what your right hand is doing.
Then your giving will be in secret. And your Father, who sees what is done in secret, will reward you.

And when you pray, do not be like the pretenders. They love to stand and pray in the synagogues and on the street corners, so that others will see them. I tell you the truth, they already have their full reward.
But when you pray, go into your room, close the door, and pray to your Father, who cannot be seen. And your Father, who sees what is done in secret, will reward you.
When you pray, do not keep saying the same things over and over, as those who do not know God do. They think they will be heard because they use many words.
Do not be like them, because your Father knows what you need before you ask him.
This is how you should pray: Our Father in heaven, may your name be honored.
May your kingdom come. May what you want be done on earth, just as it is done in heaven.
Give us today the food we need.
Forgive us our sins, as we forgive those who sin against us.
Keep us from being tempted, and save us from the evil one. For the kingdom, the power, and the glory are yours forever. Amen.
If you forgive others when they sin against you, your Father in heaven will also forgive you.
But if you do not forgive others their sins, your Father will not forgive your sins.
When you fast, do not look sad like the pretenders. They make their faces look gloomy so that others can see they are fasting. I tell you the truth, they already have their full reward.
But when you fast, comb your hair and wash your face,
so that it will not be obvious to others that you are fasting, but only to your Father, who cannot be seen. And your Father, who sees what is done in secret, will reward you.
Do not store up treasures for yourselves here on earth, where moths and rust destroy them, and where thieves break in and steal.
Instead, store up treasures for yourselves in heaven, where moths and rust do not destroy, and where thieves do not break in and steal.
For wherever your treasure is, there your heart will be also.
The eye is like a lamp for the body. If your eyes are good, your whole body will be full of light.
But if your eyes are bad, your whole body will be full of darkness. And if the light inside you is really darkness, how great that darkness is!
No one can serve two masters. He will either hate the one and love the other, or be loyal to the one and look down on the other. You cannot serve both God and money.
So I tell you, do not worry about your life — about what you will eat or drink, or about your body and what you will wear. Is not life more than food, and the body more than clothes?
Look at the birds in the sky. They do not plant or harvest or store food in barns, yet your Father in heaven feeds them. Are you not worth much more than they are?
Can any of you add a single hour to your life by worrying?
And why do you worry about clothes? Look at how the wild flowers grow. They do not work or make clothing.
But I tell you that not even Solomon in all his glory was dressed as beautifully as one of these.
If God dresses the grass of the field so well — grass that is here today and thrown into the fire tomorrow — will he not much more clothe you? You have so little faith!
So do not worry, saying: What will we eat? or, What will we drink? or, What will we wear?
The people who do not know God run after all these things. But your Father in heaven knows that you need them.
Instead, put God's kingdom first, and do what he wants, and all these things will be given to you as well.
So do not worry about tomorrow, because tomorrow will worry about itself. Each day has enough trouble of its own.
If you judge others, you will be judged by God the same way you judge them. God will treat you the same way you treat others.
Why do you see the small piece of wood in your brother’s eye, but you do not see that there is a big piece of wood in your own eye?
You can not say to your brother that you want to take out the small piece of wood in his eye, when you have a big piece of wood in your own eye.
You are a hypocrite. You must first take out the big piece of wood from your own eye. Then you will be able to take out the small piece of wood from your brother’s eye.
You must not throw things that belong to God to the dogs. And you must not throw your pearls in front of the pigs. The pigs will trample the pearls with their feet and run after you.
Ask and keep on asking and it will be given to you; seek and keep on seeking and you will find; knock and keep on knocking and the door will be opened to you.
Yes, whoever continues to ask will receive. Whoever continues to look will find. And whoever continues to knock will have the door opened for them.
When a boy asks his father for bread, his father will never give his son a stone.
And when a boy asks his father for a fish, his father will never give his son a snake.
You people are so bad, but you still know how to give good things to your children. So surely your heavenly Father will give good things to those who ask him.
Do for others what you would want them to do for you. This is the meaning of the Law of Moses and the teaching of the prophets.
Jesus also said: You must go in through the small gate. The other gate, the gate to hell, is big and wide and it is easy to walk on that road. Many people go through that big gate.
But the gate to eternal life is small and it is not easy to walk on this road. There are only a few people who find this road and walk on it.’
Be careful of false prophets. They come to you and look gentle like sheep. But they are really dangerous like wolves.
You can identify them by the way they act. Can you pick grapes from thornbushes, or figs from thistles? Matthew 7: 17 A good tree always has good fruit and a bad tree always has bad fruit.
A good tree can not have bad fruit and a bad tree can not have good fruit.
Every tree that has bad fruit will be cut down and people will throw that tree into the fire.
You will know these false people by what they do.
Not everyone who calls me Lord will enter God’s kingdom. The only people who will enter are those who do what my Father in heaven wants.
On that last Day many will call me Lord. They will say, Lord, Lord, by the power of your name we spoke for God. And by your name we forced out demons and did many miracles.
Then I will tell those people clearly, Get away from me, you people who do wrong. I never knew you.
Jesus said: Whoever hears these teachings of mine and obeys them is like a wise man who built his house on rock.
The rain came down and the rivers were full of water. The wind blew hard and it started to shake the house. But it did not fall because it was built on rock.
Whoever hears these teachings of mine and does not obey them is like a foolish man who built his house on sand.
The rain came down and the rivers became full of water. The wind blew hard and it started to shake the house and destroyed it. There was nothing left of it.
When Jesus finished talking to the people, all of them were amazed by what He taught them,
because they knew his words came from God. Jesus did not teach them like the teachers of the Laws did.

8.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: When Jesus came down from the mountain, large crowds followed him.
A man with a skin disease came and knelt before him. He said: Lord, if you are willing, you can make me clean.
Jesus reached out his hand and touched the man. He said: I am willing. Be clean! At once the man was healed of his disease.
Then Jesus said to him: Do not tell anyone. Go, show yourself to the priest, and offer the gift that Moses commanded. This will be proof to the people.
When Jesus came into Capernaum, an army officer came and begged him for help.
He said: Lord, my servant is lying at home, unable to move and suffering terribly.
Jesus said to him: I will come and heal him.
The officer answered: Lord, I am not worthy to have you come into my house. Just say the word, and my servant will be healed.
For I am a man under authority, with soldiers under me. I tell one, Go, and he goes. I tell another, Come, and he comes. I tell my servant, Do this, and he does it.
When Jesus heard this, he was amazed. He said to those following him: I tell you the truth, I have not found anyone in Israel with such great faith.
I tell you, many will come from the east and the west and will sit down with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob in the kingdom of heaven.
But those who should belong to the kingdom will be thrown outside into the darkness, where there will be crying and grinding of teeth.
Then Jesus said to the officer: Go. It will be done for you just as you believed. And his servant was healed at that very hour.
When Jesus came into Peter's house, he saw Peter's mother-in-law lying in bed with a fever.
He touched her hand, and the fever left her. She got up and began to serve him.
When evening came, people brought to Jesus many who had demons. He drove out the spirits with a word and healed all who were sick.
This happened so that the words of the prophet Isaiah would come true: He took our weaknesses and carried our diseases.
When Jesus saw the crowd around him, he gave orders to cross to the other side of the lake.
Then a teacher of the law came and said to him: Teacher, I will follow you wherever you go.
Jesus replied: Foxes have dens and birds have nests, but the Son of Man has no place to lay his head.
Another of his disciples said to him: Lord, first let me go and bury my father.
But Jesus told him: Follow me, and let the dead bury their own dead.
Then Jesus got into a boat, and his disciples followed him.
Suddenly a terrible storm came up on the lake, so that the waves swept over the boat. But Jesus was sleeping.
The disciples went and woke him, saying: Lord, save us! We are going to drown!
He replied: Why are you afraid? You have so little faith! Then he got up and ordered the winds and the waves to stop, and it became completely calm.
The men were amazed. They asked: What kind of man is this? Even the winds and the waves obey him!
When Jesus came to the other side, to the area of the Gadarenes, two men with demons came out from the tombs to meet him. They were so dangerous that no one could go that way.
They shouted: What do you want with us, Son of God? Have you come here to torture us before the appointed time?
Some distance away a large herd of pigs was feeding.
The demons begged Jesus: If you drive us out, send us into the herd of pigs.
He said to them: Go! So they came out and went into the pigs, and the whole herd rushed down the steep hill into the lake and drowned in the water.
The men who had been taking care of the pigs ran away. They went into the town and told everything, including what had happened to the men who had the demons.
Then the whole town went out to meet Jesus. When they saw him, they begged him to leave their area.

9.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Jesus got into a boat, crossed over, and came to his own town.
Some men brought to him a paralyzed man lying on a mat. When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralyzed man: Cheer up, son. Your sins are forgiven.
At this, some of the teachers of the law said to themselves: This man is insulting God.
Knowing what they were thinking, Jesus said: Why do you have evil thoughts in your hearts?
Which is easier — to say, Your sins are forgiven, or to say, Get up and walk?
But I will show you that the Son of Man has power on earth to forgive sins. So he said to the paralyzed man: Get up, take your mat, and go home.
The man got up and went home.
When the crowds saw this, they were amazed and praised God, who had given such power to people.
As Jesus went on from there, he saw a man named Matthew sitting at the tax booth. Jesus said to him: Follow me. Matthew got up and followed him.
Later, while Jesus was eating in the house, many tax collectors and sinners came and ate with him and his disciples.
When the Pharisees saw this, they asked his disciples: Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and sinners?
Jesus heard this and said: Healthy people do not need a doctor. Sick people do.
Go and learn what this means: I want mercy, not sacrifice. I did not come to invite good people, but sinners.
Then John's disciples came to Jesus and asked: We and the Pharisees fast often. Why do your disciples not fast?
Jesus answered: How can the friends of the groom be sad while he is still with them? But the time will come when the groom will be taken away from them, and then they will fast.
No one sews a piece of new cloth on an old coat, because the patch will pull away from the coat, and the tear will be worse.

People do not pour new wine into old wineskins. If they do, the skins will burst, the wine will spill out, and the skins will be ruined. Instead, they pour new wine into new wineskins, and both are kept safe.
While Jesus was saying this, a leader came and knelt before him. He said: My daughter has just died. But come and put your hand on her, and she will live.
So Jesus got up and went with him, and so did his disciples.
Just then a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years came up behind Jesus and touched the edge of his coat.
She said to herself: If I only touch his coat, I will be healed.
Jesus turned and saw her. He said: Cheer up, daughter. Your faith has healed you. And the woman was healed from that moment.
When Jesus came into the leader's house and saw the flute players and the noisy crowd,
he said: Go away. The girl is not dead. She is only sleeping. But they laughed at him.
After the crowd had been sent outside, Jesus went in and took the girl by the hand, and she got up.
News about this spread through that whole area.
As Jesus went on from there, two blind men followed him, calling out: Son of David, have mercy on us!
When Jesus went indoors, the blind men came to him. He asked them: Do you believe that I am able to do this? They answered: Yes, Lord.
Then he touched their eyes and said: It will happen to you because of your faith.
And their eyes were opened. Jesus warned them sternly: See that no one finds out about this.
But they went out and spread the news about him all over that area.
While they were leaving, some people brought to Jesus a man who could not speak because he had a demon.
When the demon was driven out, the man began to speak. The crowds were amazed and said: Nothing like this has ever been seen in Israel.
But the Pharisees said: He drives out demons by the power of the prince of demons.
Jesus went through all the towns and villages, teaching in their synagogues, preaching the good news of the kingdom, and healing every kind of sickness and disease.
When he saw the crowds, he felt sorry for them, because they were troubled and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
Then he said to his disciples: The harvest is large, but the workers are few.
So ask the Lord of the harvest to send out workers into his harvest field.

10.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Jesus called his twelve disciples to him and gave them power to drive out evil spirits and to heal every kind of sickness and disease.
These are the names of the twelve apostles: first, Simon (who is called Peter) and his brother Andrew; James son of Zebedee and his brother John;
Philip and Bartholomew; Thomas and Matthew the tax collector; James son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus;
Simon the Zealot, and Judas Iscariot, who later handed Jesus over to his enemies.
Jesus sent out these twelve with these instructions: Do not go among the other nations or enter any town of the Samaritans.
Go instead to the lost sheep of Israel.
As you go, preach this message: The kingdom of heaven is near.
Heal the sick, raise the dead, cure those with skin diseases, and drive out demons. You received freely, so give freely.
Do not take any gold, silver, or copper in your belts.
Take no bag for the trip, no extra shirt, no sandals, and no walking stick, because a worker deserves to be fed.
When you enter a town or village, find someone there who is worthy, and stay at his house until you leave.
When you go into the house, give it your greeting of peace.
If the home is worthy, let your peace rest on it. If it is not worthy, let your peace come back to you.
If anyone will not welcome you or listen to your words, shake the dust off your feet when you leave that home or town.
I tell you the truth, on the day of judgment it will be easier for Sodom and Gomorrah than for that town.
Look, I am sending you out like sheep among wolves. So be as wise as snakes and as innocent as doves.
Be on your guard against people. They will hand you over to the courts and beat you in their synagogues.
Because of me, you will be brought before governors and kings to tell them and the other nations the truth.
But when they arrest you, do not worry about how to speak or what to say. At that time you will be given what to say,
because it will not be you speaking, but the Spirit of your Father speaking through you.
Brother will hand over brother to be killed, and a father will hand over his child. Children will turn against their parents and have them put to death.
Everyone will hate you because of me, but the one who stands strong to the end will be saved.
When they treat you badly in one town, run to another. I tell you the truth, you will not finish going through the towns of Israel before the Son of Man comes.
A student is not greater than his teacher, and a servant is not greater than his master.
It is enough for the student to be like his teacher, and the servant like his master. If the head of the house has been called Beelzebub, how much more will they insult the members of his household!
So do not be afraid of them. There is nothing hidden that will not be brought out into the open, and nothing secret that will not be made known.
What I tell you in the dark, say in the daylight. What is whispered in your ear, shout from the housetops.
Do not be afraid of those who kill the body but cannot kill the soul. Instead, be afraid of the One who can destroy both soul and body in hell.
Are not two sparrows sold for a penny? Yet not one of them falls to the ground apart from your Father.
And even the hairs of your head are all numbered.
So do not be afraid. You are worth more than many sparrows.
Whoever says in front of others that he belongs to me, I will also say in front of my Father in heaven that he belongs to me.
But whoever says in front of others that he does not know me, I will also say in front of my Father in heaven that I do not know him.
Do not think that I came to bring peace to the earth. I did not come to bring peace, but a sword.
For I have come to turn a man against his father, a daughter against her mother, and a daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law.
A person's enemies will be the members of his own household.
Anyone who loves his father or mother more than me is not worthy of me. Anyone who loves his son or daughter more than me is not worthy of me.
And anyone who does not take up his cross and follow me is not worthy of me.
Whoever tries to save his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life for me will find it.
Anyone who welcomes you welcomes me, and anyone who welcomes me welcomes the One who sent me.
Whoever welcomes a prophet because he is a prophet will receive a prophet's reward. And whoever welcomes a good person because he is good will receive a good person's reward.
And if anyone gives even a cup of cold water to one of these little ones because he is my follower, I tell you the truth, he will surely be rewarded.

11.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: After Jesus finished instructing his twelve disciples, he went on from there to teach and preach in the towns of Galilee.
John was in prison, and when he heard about the things Christ was doing, he sent his disciples
to ask him: Are you the one who is to come, or should we look for someone else?
Jesus answered: Go back and tell John what you hear and see:
The blind can see, the crippled can walk, those with skin diseases are made clean, the deaf can hear, the dead are raised to life, and the good news is preached to the poor.
And blessed is anyone who does not turn away because of me.
As John's disciples were leaving, Jesus began to speak to the crowds about John: What did you go out into the desert to see? A reed shaken by the wind?
If not that, what did you go out to see? A man dressed in fine clothes? No, people who wear fine clothes live in kings' palaces.
Then what did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
John is the one the Scriptures speak about: I am sending my messenger ahead of you. He will prepare your way.
I tell you the truth, no one greater than John the Baptist has ever been born. Yet the one who is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he is.
From the days of John the Baptist until now, the kingdom of heaven has been under attack, and violent people try to take it by force.
For all the prophets and the law of Moses spoke about these things until John came.
And if you are willing to accept it, John is the Elijah who was to come.
Anyone who has ears should listen.
To what can I compare the people of this time? They are like children sitting in the marketplaces, calling out to the others:
We played the flute for you, but you would not dance. We sang a sad song, but you would not cry.
For John came neither eating nor drinking, and people say: He has a demon.
The Son of Man came eating and drinking, and people say: Look at him — he eats and drinks too much, and he is a friend of tax collectors and sinners. But wisdom is shown to be right by what it does.
Then Jesus began to speak against the towns where he had done most of his miracles, because the people there had not turned away from their sins.

How terrible it will be for you, Chorazin! How terrible for you, Bethsaida! If the miracles done in you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, those people would have turned from their sins long ago, dressing in rough cloth and sitting in ashes.
But I tell you, on the day of judgment it will be easier for Tyre and Sidon than for you.
And you, Capernaum, will you be lifted up to heaven? No, you will go down to the place of the dead. If the miracles done in you had been done in Sodom, it would still be here today.
But I tell you, on the day of judgment it will be easier for Sodom than for you.
At that time Jesus said: I praise you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because you have hidden these things from the wise and the educated, and have shown them to little children.
Yes, Father, this is what you wanted.
My Father has handed over everything to me. No one knows the Son except the Father, and no one knows the Father except the Son and those to whom the Son chooses to show him.
Come to me, all of you who are tired and carrying heavy loads, and I will give you rest.
Take my yoke on you and learn from me, because I am gentle and humble in heart, and you will find rest for your souls.
For my yoke is easy, and the load I give you is light.

12.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: About that time Jesus was walking through the grain fields on the Sabbath. His disciples were hungry, so they began to pick some heads of grain and eat them.
When the Pharisees saw this, they said to him: Look! Your disciples are doing what is not allowed on the Sabbath.
Jesus answered: Have you not read what David did when he and his men were hungry?
He went into the house of God, and they ate the holy bread, which was not allowed for him or his men to eat, but only for the priests.
Or have you not read in the law that on the Sabbath the priests in the temple break the Sabbath rule and yet are not guilty?
I tell you that something greater than the temple is here.
If you had known what this means — I want mercy, not sacrifice — you would not have judged these innocent men guilty.
For the Son of Man is Lord of the Sabbath.
Jesus went on from there and entered their synagogue.
A man with a crippled hand was there. Looking for a reason to accuse Jesus, they asked him: Is it right to heal on the Sabbath?
He said to them: If any of you has a sheep and it falls into a pit on the Sabbath, will you not take hold of it and lift it out?
And a person is worth far more than a sheep! So it is right to do good on the Sabbath.
Then he said to the man: Hold out your hand. The man held it out, and it was made as healthy as the other one.
But the Pharisees went out and made plans to kill Jesus.
Knowing this, Jesus left that place. Many people followed him, and he healed all of them,
warning them not to tell others who he was.
This happened so that the words of the prophet Isaiah would come true:
Here is my servant, whom I have chosen, the one I love, who pleases me. I will put my Spirit on him, and he will announce justice to the nations.
He will not argue or shout, and no one will hear his voice in the streets.
He will not break a bent reed or put out a flickering candle, until he brings justice to victory.
And the nations will put their hope in his name.
Then they brought to Jesus a man who had a demon and was blind and could not speak. Jesus healed him, so that he could both speak and see.
All the people were amazed and said: Could this be the Son of David?
But when the Pharisees heard this, they said: This man drives out demons only by Beelzebub, the prince of demons.
Jesus knew what they were thinking and said to them: Every kingdom that fights against itself will be destroyed, and every city or family that fights against itself will not stand.
If Satan drives out Satan, he is fighting against himself. How then can his kingdom stand?
And if I drive out demons by Beelzebub, by whom do your own people drive them out? So they will be your judges.
But if I drive out demons by the Spirit of God, then the kingdom of God has come to you.
How can anyone go into a strong man's house and steal his things unless he first ties up the strong man? Then he can rob his house.
Whoever is not with me is against me, and whoever does not gather with me scatters.
So I tell you, people can be forgiven for every sin and insult against God. But the insult against the Holy Spirit will not be forgiven.
Anyone who speaks a word against the Son of Man can be forgiven. But anyone who speaks against the Holy Spirit will not be forgiven, either in this world or in the world to come.
Make a tree good, and its fruit will be good. Make a tree bad, and its fruit will be bad. A tree is known by its fruit.
You family of snakes! How can you say anything good when you are evil? For the mouth speaks what the heart is full of.
A good person brings good things out of the good stored up in him, and an evil person brings evil things out of the evil stored up in him.
I tell you, on the day of judgment people will have to give an account for every careless word they have spoken.
By your words you will be cleared, and by your words you will be found guilty.
Then some of the teachers of the law and the Pharisees said to him: Teacher, we want to see a miraculous sign from you.
Jesus answered: Only an evil and unfaithful people look for a sign. But none will be given to them except the sign of the prophet Jonah.
For just as Jonah was three days and three nights in the belly of the huge fish, so the Son of Man will be three days and three nights in the heart of the earth.

The people of Nineveh will stand up at the judgment with the people of this time and will say they are guilty. For the people of Nineveh turned from their sins when Jonah preached, and now something greater than Jonah is here.

The Queen of the South will stand up at the judgment with the people of this time and will say they are guilty. For she came from the ends of the earth to listen to Solomon's wisdom, and now something greater than Solomon is here.
When an evil spirit comes out of a person, it travels through dry places looking for rest and does not find any.
Then it says: I will go back to the home I left. When it returns, it finds the house empty, swept clean, and in order.

Then it goes and brings with it seven other spirits more evil than itself, and they go in and live there. And that person ends up worse than before. That is how it will be with the evil people of this time.
While Jesus was still talking to the crowd, his mother and brothers stood outside, wanting to speak to him.
Someone told him: Your mother and brothers are standing outside, wanting to speak to you.
Jesus answered: Who is my mother, and who are my brothers?
Then he pointed to his disciples and said: Here are my mother and my brothers.
For whoever does what my Father in heaven wants is my brother and sister and mother.

13.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: That same day Jesus went out of the house and sat by the lake.
Such large crowds gathered around him that he got into a boat and sat down, while all the people stood on the shore.
Then he told them many things using word pictures. He said: A farmer went out to plant his seed.
As he scattered the seed, some fell on the path, and the birds came and ate it up.
Some fell on rocky ground, where there was not much soil. It grew quickly, because the soil was not deep.
But when the sun came up, the plants were scorched, and they dried up because they had no roots.
Some seed fell among thorns, and the thorns grew up and choked the plants.
But some seed fell on good soil, where it produced a crop — a hundred, sixty, or thirty times what was planted.
Anyone who has ears should listen.
The disciples came to Jesus and asked: Why do you speak to the people in word pictures?
He answered: You have been allowed to know the secrets of the kingdom of heaven, but they have not.
Those who have something will be given more, and they will have plenty. But those who have nothing will lose even what they have.
This is why I speak to them in word pictures: Though they see, they do not really see; though they hear, they do not really hear or understand.
In them the words of the prophet Isaiah come true: You will hear and hear, but never understand. You will look and look, but never see.

For these people have become stubborn. They hardly hear with their ears, and they have closed their eyes. Otherwise they might see with their eyes, hear with their ears, understand with their hearts, turn to me, and be healed.
But blessed are your eyes, because they see, and your ears, because they hear.
I tell you the truth, many prophets and good people wanted to see what you see, but they did not see it, and to hear what you hear, but they did not hear it.
So listen to what the word picture of the farmer means.
When people hear the message about the kingdom and do not understand it, the evil one comes and snatches away what was planted in their hearts. This is the seed planted on the path.
The seed planted on rocky ground is the person who hears the message and right away receives it with joy.
But since he has no root, he lasts only a short time. When trouble or suffering comes because of the message, he quickly gives up.
The seed planted among thorns is the person who hears the message, but the worries of this life and the love of money choke it, so that it produces nothing.
But the seed planted on good soil is the person who hears the message and understands it. He produces a crop — a hundred, sixty, or thirty times what was planted.
Jesus told them another word picture: The kingdom of heaven is like a man who planted good seed in his field.
But while everyone was sleeping, his enemy came and planted weeds among the wheat, and then went away.
When the wheat came up and formed heads, the weeds also appeared.
The owner's servants came to him and said: Sir, did you not plant good seed in your field? Where did the weeds come from?
He answered: An enemy did this. The servants asked: Do you want us to go and pull up the weeds?
No, he said, because while you are pulling up the weeds, you might pull up the wheat with them.
Let both grow together until the harvest. At that time I will tell the workers: First gather the weeds and tie them in bundles to be burned. Then gather the wheat into my barn.
Jesus told them another word picture: The kingdom of heaven is like a mustard seed that a man planted in his field.
It is the smallest of all seeds, but when it grows, it is the largest of the garden plants and becomes a tree, so that the birds come and rest in its branches.
He told them still another word picture: The kingdom of heaven is like yeast that a woman mixed into a large amount of flour until it worked all through the dough.
Jesus said all these things to the crowds using word pictures. He did not say anything to them without using a word picture.
This happened so that the words of the prophet would come true: I will open my mouth in word pictures. I will tell things that have been kept secret since the creation of the world.
Then Jesus left the crowd and went into the house. His disciples came to him and said: Explain to us the word picture about the weeds in the field.
He answered: The one who plants the good seed is the Son of Man.
The field is the world, and the good seed stands for the people of the kingdom. The weeds are the people who belong to the evil one,
and the enemy who plants them is the devil. The harvest is the end of the world, and the workers are the angels.
Just as the weeds are pulled up and burned in the fire, so it will be at the end of the world.
The Son of Man will send out his angels, and they will gather out of his kingdom everything that causes sin and all who do evil.
They will throw them into the blazing furnace, where there will be crying and grinding of teeth.
Then the good people will shine like the sun in the kingdom of their Father. Anyone who has ears should listen.
The kingdom of heaven is like treasure hidden in a field. When a man found it, he hid it again. Then in his joy he went and sold everything he had and bought that field.
Again, the kingdom of heaven is like a merchant looking for fine pearls.
When he found one very valuable pearl, he went and sold everything he had and bought it.
Again, the kingdom of heaven is like a net that was let down into the lake and caught all kinds of fish.
When it was full, the fishermen pulled it up on the shore. Then they sat down and put the good fish into baskets, but threw the bad fish away.
This is how it will be at the end of the world. The angels will come and separate the wicked people from the good
and throw them into the blazing furnace, where there will be crying and grinding of teeth.
Jesus asked: Have you understood all these things? They answered: Yes.
He said to them: So every teacher of the law who has been taught about the kingdom of heaven is like the owner of a house who brings out of his storeroom both new treasures and old.
When Jesus had finished these word pictures, he went on from there.
He came to his hometown and began teaching the people in their synagogue. They were amazed and asked: Where did this man get this wisdom and these miracles?
Is this not the carpenter's son? Is not his mother named Mary, and are not his brothers James, Joseph, Simon, and Judas?
Are not all his sisters here with us? Where then did this man get all these things?
And they were not willing to accept him. But Jesus said to them: A prophet is honored everywhere except in his hometown and in his own home.
And he did not do many miracles there because of their lack of faith.

14.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: About that time Herod the ruler heard the reports about Jesus.
He said to his servants: This is John the Baptist. He has come back from the dead, and that is why he has the power to do miracles.
Herod had arrested John, tied him up, and put him in prison because of Herodias, the wife of his brother Philip.
John had been telling Herod: It is not right for you to have her.
Herod wanted to kill John, but he was afraid of the people, because they believed John was a prophet.
On Herod's birthday, the daughter of Herodias danced for the guests and pleased Herod.
So he promised with an oath to give her anything she asked for.
Her mother told her what to say, so she said: Give me right here the head of John the Baptist on a plate.
The king was sad, but because of his oath and his guests, he ordered that her wish be granted.
He sent men to behead John in the prison.
John's head was brought in on a plate and given to the girl, who took it to her mother.
John's disciples came and took his body and buried it. Then they went and told Jesus.
When Jesus heard what had happened, he went away in a boat to a lonely place to be alone. But the crowds heard about it and followed him on foot from the towns.
When Jesus came ashore and saw the large crowd, he felt sorry for them and healed their sick.
As evening came, his disciples came to him and said: This is a lonely place, and it is already late. Send the people away so they can go to the villages and buy food for themselves.
Jesus replied: They do not need to go away. You give them something to eat.
They said to him: All we have here is five loaves of bread and two fish.
He said: Bring them here to me.

He told the people to sit down on the grass. Then he took the five loaves and the two fish, looked up to heaven, and gave thanks. He broke the loaves and gave them to the disciples, and the disciples gave them to the people.
Everyone ate and was full, and the disciples picked up twelve baskets of leftover pieces.
About five thousand men ate, besides women and children.
Right away Jesus made his disciples get into the boat and go ahead of him to the other side, while he sent the people away.
After he had sent them away, he went up on a mountain by himself to pray. When evening came, he was there alone.
By now the boat was far from land, tossed about by the waves, because the wind was against it.
Some time before dawn, Jesus came to the disciples, walking on the water.
When they saw him walking on the water, they were terrified. They said: It is a ghost! And they cried out in fear.
But right away Jesus said to them: Be brave! It is I. Do not be afraid.
Peter answered: Lord, if it is you, tell me to come to you on the water.
Jesus said: Come. So Peter got out of the boat and walked on the water toward Jesus.
But when Peter saw the strong wind, he was afraid. As he began to sink, he cried out: Lord, save me!
At once Jesus reached out his hand and caught him. He said: Your faith is so small. Why did you doubt?
And when they climbed into the boat, the wind stopped.
Then those in the boat worshiped Jesus, saying: You really are the Son of God.
When they had crossed over, they came to land at Gennesaret.
When the people there recognized Jesus, they sent word to the whole area around them, and the people brought him all who were sick.
They begged him to let the sick just touch the edge of his coat, and all who touched it were healed.

15.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Then some Pharisees and teachers of the law came to Jesus from Jerusalem and asked:
Why do your disciples break the teaching of the elders? They do not wash their hands before they eat.
Jesus answered: And why do you break the command of God for the sake of your own teaching?
For God said: Honor your father and mother. And: Anyone who curses his father or mother must be put to death.
But you say that a person may tell his father or mother: Whatever help you might have received from me is now a gift given to God.
In that way he does not have to honor his father or mother. So you cancel the word of God for the sake of your own teaching.
You pretenders! Isaiah was right when he spoke about you:
These people honor me with their lips, but their hearts are far away from me.
Their worship of me means nothing. They teach human rules as if they were God's commands.
Jesus called the crowd to him and said: Listen and understand.
It is not what goes into the mouth that makes a person unclean. It is what comes out of the mouth that makes a person unclean.
Then the disciples came to him and said: Do you know that the Pharisees were offended when they heard this?
He answered: Every plant that my heavenly Father has not planted will be pulled up by the roots.
Leave them alone. They are blind guides. And if a blind person leads a blind person, both will fall into a pit.
Peter said: Explain this word picture to us.
Jesus asked: Do you still not understand?
Don't you see that whatever goes into the mouth goes into the stomach and then passes out of the body?
But the things that come out of the mouth come from the heart, and these make a person unclean.
For out of the heart come evil thoughts, murder, adultery, sexual sin, stealing, lying, and insulting God.
These are the things that make a person unclean. But eating with unwashed hands does not make a person unclean.
Then Jesus left that place and went away to the area of Tyre and Sidon.
A woman from Canaan who lived in that area came to him, crying out: Lord, Son of David, have mercy on me! My daughter is suffering terribly from a demon.
Jesus did not answer her a word. So his disciples came and begged him: Send her away, for she keeps crying out after us.
He answered: I was sent only to the lost sheep of Israel.
But the woman came and knelt before him. She said: Lord, help me!
He replied: It is not right to take the children's bread and throw it to the dogs.
She said: Yes, Lord, but even the dogs eat the crumbs that fall from their masters' table.
Then Jesus answered her: Woman, your faith is great! What you want will be done for you. And her daughter was healed at that very hour.
Jesus left there and went along the Sea of Galilee. Then he went up on a mountain and sat down.
Large crowds came to him, bringing the lame, the blind, those who could not speak, the crippled, and many others. They laid them at Jesus' feet, and he healed them.
The people were amazed when they saw those who could not speak now speaking, the crippled made well, the lame walking, and the blind seeing. And they praised the God of Israel.

Then Jesus called his disciples to him and said: I feel sorry for these people. They have been with me three days now and have nothing to eat. I do not want to send them away hungry, or they may faint on the way.
His disciples answered: Where could we get enough bread in this lonely place to feed such a large crowd?
Jesus asked: How many loaves do you have? They answered: Seven, and a few small fish.
He told the crowd to sit down on the ground.
Then he took the seven loaves and the fish, gave thanks, broke them, and gave them to the disciples, and the disciples gave them to the people.
They all ate and were full. Afterward the disciples picked up seven baskets of leftover pieces.
Four thousand men ate, besides women and children.
After Jesus had sent the crowd away, he got into the boat and went to the area of Magadan.

16.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: The Pharisees and Sadducees came to test Jesus. They asked him to show them a sign from heaven.
He answered: In the evening you say, The weather will be good, because the sky is red.
And in the morning you say, It will be stormy today, because the sky is red and cloudy. You know how to read the look of the sky, but you cannot read the signs of the times.
Only an evil and unfaithful people look for a sign. But none will be given to them except the sign of Jonah. Then Jesus left them and went away.
When the disciples crossed to the other side of the lake, they forgot to bring bread.
Jesus said to them: Be careful. Watch out for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
They talked about this among themselves and said: He says this because we did not bring any bread.
Jesus knew what they were saying, so he said: You have so little faith. Why are you talking about having no bread?
Do you still not understand? Don't you remember the five loaves for the five thousand, and how many baskets you picked up?
Or the seven loaves for the four thousand, and how many baskets you picked up?
How is it that you do not understand? I was not talking to you about bread. I told you to watch out for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
Then they understood that he was not telling them to watch out for the yeast used in bread, but for the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
When Jesus came to the area of Caesarea Philippi, he asked his disciples: Who do people say the Son of Man is?
They answered: Some say John the Baptist. Others say Elijah, and still others say Jeremiah or one of the prophets.
But what about you? he asked. Who do you say I am?
Simon Peter answered: You are the Christ, the Son of the living God.
Jesus replied: God has blessed you, Simon son of Jonah. No person showed this to you, but my Father in heaven did.
And I tell you, you are Peter, and on this rock I will build my church, and the powers of death will not defeat it.
I will give you the keys of the kingdom of heaven. Whatever you do not allow on earth will not be allowed in heaven, and whatever you allow on earth will be allowed in heaven.
Then Jesus warned his disciples not to tell anyone that he was the Christ.

From that time on, Jesus began to explain to his disciples that he had to go to Jerusalem and suffer many things at the hands of the elders, the chief priests, and the teachers of the law. He would be killed, and on the third day he would be raised to life.
Peter took him aside and began to correct him. He said: Never, Lord! This will never happen to you!
But Jesus turned and said to Peter: Get behind me, Satan! You are getting in my way. You are not thinking about the things of God, but about the things of people.
Then Jesus said to his disciples: If anyone wants to follow me, he must say no to himself, take up his cross, and follow me.
For whoever wants to save his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life for me will find it.
What good is it for someone to gain the whole world but lose his soul? Or what can a person give to buy back his soul?
For the Son of Man is going to come in his Father's glory with his angels, and then he will reward each person according to what he has done.
I tell you the truth, some who are standing here will not die before they see the Son of Man coming in his kingdom.

17.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Six days later Jesus took Peter, James, and his brother John and led them up a high mountain by themselves.
There Jesus was changed in front of them. His face shone like the sun, and his clothes became as white as light.
Suddenly Moses and Elijah appeared, talking with Jesus.
Peter said to Jesus: Lord, it is good for us to be here. If you want, I will put up three shelters — one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.
While he was still speaking, a bright cloud covered them, and a voice from the cloud said: This is my Son, whom I love. I am very pleased with him. Listen to him!
When the disciples heard this, they fell facedown to the ground, terrified.
But Jesus came and touched them. He said: Get up. Do not be afraid.
When they looked up, they saw no one except Jesus.
As they were coming down the mountain, Jesus told them: Do not tell anyone what you have seen, until the Son of Man has been raised from the dead.
The disciples asked him: Why do the teachers of the law say that Elijah must come first?
Jesus replied: Elijah does come, and he will make all things ready.
But I tell you, Elijah has already come, and they did not recognize him. Instead, they did to him whatever they wanted. In the same way, the Son of Man is going to suffer at their hands.
Then the disciples understood that he was talking about John the Baptist.
When they came back to the crowd, a man came up to Jesus and knelt before him.
He said: Lord, have mercy on my son. He has seizures and suffers terribly. He often falls into the fire or into the water.
I brought him to your disciples, but they could not heal him.
Jesus answered: You people have no faith, and your hearts are turned away from God. How long must I stay with you? How long must I put up with you? Bring the boy here to me.
Jesus ordered the demon out, and it left the boy. He was healed at that very hour.
Then the disciples came to Jesus in private and asked: Why couldn't we drive it out?

He answered: Because your faith is too small. I tell you the truth, if you have faith as small as a mustard seed, you can say to this mountain, Move from here to there, and it will move. Nothing will be impossible for you.
But this kind comes out only by prayer and fasting.
While they were together in Galilee, Jesus said to them: The Son of Man is going to be handed over to men.
They will kill him, and on the third day he will be raised to life. And the disciples were filled with sorrow.
When they came to Capernaum, the men who collected the temple tax came to Peter and asked: Does your teacher not pay the temple tax?

Yes, he does, Peter answered. When Peter came into the house, Jesus spoke first. He asked: What do you think, Simon? From whom do the kings of the earth collect taxes — from their own children, or from others?
From others, Peter answered. Jesus said to him: Then the children are free.

But so that we do not offend them, go to the lake and throw in a hook. Take the first fish you catch, and when you open its mouth, you will find a coin. Take it and give it to them for my tax and yours.
When Jesus walked away from the temple, his disciples came to Him and the disciples showed Jesus the buildings of the temple.
Jesus said to the disciples: Do you see these buildings? The buildings will be destroyed. Every stone will be thrown down to the ground. Not one stone will be left on another.

Later when Jesus sat on the Mount of Olives, Jesus disciples came to Him when they were alone with Him. The disciples asked Jesus: Tell us: When will these things happen that You have told us about? How will we know when it will happen? And what will happen to prepare us for your coming and the end of time
Jesus said to his disciples: You must be careful that no person tells you lies.
Many people will use my name and say that they are the Christ. They will deceive many people who will believe them.
Do not be afraid when you hear there is war near you or a war far from you. These things must happen, but that will not be the end of everything.

One nation will fight against another nation, and the people of one land will make war against the people of another land. There will be many diseases and earthquakes in many places and people will have no food to eat.
This will be only the beginning of the suffering.
People will arrest and punish you. They will beat you and they will kill some of you. People of all nations will hate you because you believe in Jesus.
During that time many believers will lose their faith. They will turn against each other and hate each other.
There will be many false prophets and they will teach things that are wrong to the people, and the false prophets will deceive the people.
There will be so much more evil in the world that the love of most will grow cold
But if someone continues to follow Jesus, then God will save the one who believes.
People who believe will bring this message to all the people on earth. All the nations will hear it and only then will it be the end of the world.
Jesus said: You will see a false god sacrifices in the temple. It will make God very angry. The prophet Daniel wrote about it. (You who read this should understand what it means.)
When this happens, the people who are in Judea must run and flee to the mountains.
If someone is on the roof of his house, he must run. He must not first go into the house to get something.
And if someone is in the field, he must not go home to get his clothes.
During that time it will be hard for women who are pregnant or have small babies!
Pray that it will not be winter or a Sabbath day when these things happen and you have to run away
it will be a time of great trouble. There will be more trouble than has ever happened since the beginning of the world. And nothing as bad as that will ever happen again.
And if those days of trouble had not been cut short, no human life would be saved; but for the sake of God’s chosen ones those days will be shortened.
When someone says in that time: Look, here is the Christ, or Look, there He is, you must not believe him.

Because there will be people who are false, and they will say they are the Christ. There will also be false prophets and they will make signs and do miracles. They will try to deceive the chosen people of God to follow them.
Now I have warned you about this before it happens.
When people say: Look, the Christ is in the desert, then you must not go there. And if they say: Look, He is here in a room, you must not believe them.
When I, the Son of man, come, it will be like the lightning in the sky that you can see from the east to the west.
It is like looking for a dead body: You will find it where the eagles are gathering above.
Right after the trouble of those days, this will happen: The sun will become dark, and the moon will not give light. The stars will fall from the sky, and everything in the sky will shake.

Then there will be a sign in the sky that will show that I, the Son of man, am coming. And the people of the earth will cry and mourn when they see the Son of man coming on the clouds in the sky. I will come with power and great glory
When you hear the big trumpet, I will send my angels to bring all the chosen people of God together. They will come from the east, the west, the north and the south, from all over the world.
Jesus said: You must learn this lesson from the fig tree: When the branches of a fig tree are getting soft and the leaves start to grow, then you know that summer is near.
So, when you see all these things happen, then you will know that the Lord is near, and He will be coming soon.
I tell you this and it is sure: Some people who are alive now will still be living when these things will happen.
Heaven and earth will disappear one day, but my words will not disappear.
Jesus said: No one knows on what day and what time of day I will come again. The angels in heaven do not know when it will be. Only the Father knows.

When I, the Son of man, come again, it will be like in the days of Noah. Matthew 24;38 In those days before the flood of water came, the people were eating and drinking and getting married until the day that Noah went into the ark.
They did not know what was happening, until the flood came and the water took everything away. It will be the same when I, the Son of man, come again.
Then 2 men will be working in field and God will take one person but not the other person.
And 2 women will be grinding wheat together and God will take one woman but not the other one.
You must be ready, because you do not know when the Lord will come.
But you must know this: If the owner of a house knows when a thief will come in the night, then he will stay awake and he will not let the thief break into his house and steal his things.
That is why you must always be ready, because you do not know when I, the Son of man, will come.
Who is the wise and trusted servant? The master trusts one servant to give the other servants their food at the right time. Who is the one the master trusts to do that work?
When the master comes and finds that servant doing the work he gave him, it will be a day of blessing for that servant
I tell you this: The master will make that servant the manager of all the things of the master.
But if the master chooses a bad servant to be the manager over the other servants, that servant will think his master will stay away for a long time, or that his master will not come back.
Then he will start to beat the other servants and he will eat and drink with bad people who are always drunk.
And one day when the master of the servants comes back, he will surprise that bad servant who did not think that the master would come back on that day and at that time.
Then the master will punish that servant. He will send him away to be with the hypocrites, where people will cry and grind their teeth with pain.
Jesus said: in the future when God in heaven rules over all, will be like ten virgins who took their lamps and went to meet the bridegroom before the wedding.
Five of the girls were foolish, and five were wise.
The 5 foolish girls took their lamps, but they did not take extra oil for the lamps
The 5 wise girls took their lamps and also jars of oil.
The bridegroom was late, and all the girls became sleepy and they started to sleep.
At midnight someone shouted, the bridegroom is coming. Come out and meet him.
Then all 10 girls woke up and made their lamps ready.
The foolish girls said to the wise girls: Give us some of your oil, because our lamps are going out.
The wise girls answered, No, The oil we have might not be enough for all of us. But go to those who sell oil and buy some for yourselves.
So the foolish girls went to buy oil. While they were gone, the bridegroom came. The girls who were ready went in with the bridegroom to the wedding feast. Then the door was closed.
When they came back, the door was closed and they shouted: Sir, sir, open the door for us.
But the bridegroom said: I am telling you this and it is true: I do not know you.
Jesus then said: You must be ready, because you do not know what day and what time of the day the Son of man will come.
Jesus said: in the future when God in heaven rules over all, it is like when a man went away from his home to another country. He called his servants and told them to look after his money.
He gave 5 talents of money to the first servant, 2 talents of money to the 2nd servant and one talent of money to the 3rd servant.
The servant who got 5 talents started to buy and sell many things and he got 5 more talents, so then he had 10 talents.
The servant with 2 talents did the same and he got 2 more talents, so then he had 4 talents.
But the servant who had one talent dug a hole in the ground and he hid his master’s money.
After a long time the master of the servants came back and asked them what they had done with his money.

The servant who got 5 talents brought the 10 talents and he said: Sir, you gave me 5 talents. Here are another 5 talents that I have worked for. Matthew 25: 21 His master said: Good Job. You are a good servant who can be trusted. You did well with that small amount of money. I will let you care for much greater things. Come and share my happiness with me.
The servant who got 2 talents came with the 4 talents and he said: Sir, you gave me 2 talents. Here are another 2 talents that I have worked for.

His master said: That is good. You are a good servant. You did what I wanted. You have worked well with the little money that I gave you. Now I will give you much to work with. Come in and be happy with me, your master.
The servant who got one talent came and said: Sir, I know you are a very hard-working man. You harvest wheat that you did not plant. You cut wheat where you did not sow any seeds.
I was afraid, so I buried the money and hid it under the ground. Here is the one talent that you gave me. It belongs to you.
His master said: You are a bad and lazy servant. You know I harvest wheat where I did not plant seeds and I cut wheat where I did not sow any seeds.
Why didn’t you put my money in the bank? Then when I came home, the bank would have given me more money.
He said to his other servants: Take the money away from him and give it to the servant who has 10 talents.
I will give more to everyone who has something, then he will have a lot more. But I will take away from anyone who does not have anything. I will take away what he has.
Then the master said, throw that useless servant outside into the darkness, where people will cry and grind their teeth with pain.
Jesus said: The Son of Man will come again with greatness, and all his angels will come with him. He will sit as king on his great and glorious throne.
Then all the nations will come together before Me, and I will separate all the people into 2 groups like a shepherd separates his sheep from the goats.
I will put the sheep on my right-hand side and the goats on my left-hand side.
Then the king will say to the godly people on his right, Come, my Father has great blessings for you. The kingdom he promised is now yours. It has been prepared for you since the world was made.
When I was hungry, you gave Me food to eat and when I was thirsty, you gave Me water to drink. When I was a stranger, you welcomed Me into your house.
