I did not have any clothes and you gave Me clothes to wear. I was sick and you cared for Me. I was in jail, and you visited Me.
Then the godly people will answer, Lord, when did we see you hungry and give you food? When did we see you thirsty and give you something to drink?
When did we see that You were a stranger and when did we welcome You into our homes? When did we see that You had no clothes and when did we give You clothes to wear?
When did we see that You were sick or in jail and when did we visit You?
Then the king will answer, the truth is, anything you did for any of my people here, you also did for me.
Then the king will say to the evil people on his left, go away from Me. God has punished you. You will go into the fire that burns forever. God has made that fire for the devil and his angels.
I was hungry, you gave Me no food to eat and when I was thirsty, you gave Me no water to drink.
I was a stranger, and you did not welcome Me into your home and when I had no clothes, you did not give Me clothes to wear. I was sick and in jail and you did not come to visit Me.”

Then the people on his left-hand side will ask the King: Lord, when did we see that You were hungry or thirsty, or when were You a stranger and did not have clothes? When were You sick or in jail? When did we not help You?
The king will answer, the truth is, anything you refused to do for any of my people here, you refused to do for me.
Then these evil people will go away to be punished forever. But the godly people will go and enjoy eternal life.
The Sabbath day was over. It was Sunday morning just before the sun came up. Mary Magdalene and the other Mary went to see the tomb.
An angel of the Lord came down from heaven and there was a big earthquake. The angel went to the tomb, and he rolled the stone away and sat on the stone.
The angel looked as bright as lightning and his clothes were as white as snow.
The soldiers who guarded the tomb were afraid. They trembled and became like dead men.
The angel said to the women: Do not be afraid. I know you are looking for Jesus who was crucified.
Angel said Jesus is not here. Jesus is alive again. Jesus said He would live again. Come and look. Here is the place where his body was lying.
Go quickly and tell Jesus disciples that Jesus lives again. Tell them Jesus is going to Galilee and they must also go there. They will see Jesus there. This is what I came to tell you.
Mary Magdalene and the other Mary left the tomb quickly. They were afraid, but they were also very happy. They ran away from the tomb to tell Jesus disciples.

Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were running when Jesus stood before them, and Jesus greeted them. Mary Magdalene and the other Mary went to Jesus. They knelt before Him and touched Jesus’ feet and worshipped Him.
Then Jesus said to Mary Magdalene and the other Mary: Do not be afraid. Go and tell my disciples to go to Galilee. They will see Me there.
Mary Magdalene and the other Mary went away from the tomb, and the soldiers who had guarded the tomb went to Jerusalem. The soldiers told the chief priests everything that had happened at the tomb.
Then the chief priests met with the older Jewish leaders came together and they made plans. They gave a lot of money to the soldiers.
The chief priests and the older Jewish leaders told the soldiers: You must tell the people that the disciples of Jesus came in the night to steal his body while you were sleeping.
If the governor hears about this, we will talk to the governor and keep you soldiers out of trouble.
The soldiers then took the money, and they did what the chief priest and Jewish leaders had told them to do. The Jews still believe that this is what happened.
The 11 disciples went to the land of Galilee. Jesus had told the disciples to go to a mountain.
When the disciples saw Jesus, they knelt before Jesus and worshipped Him. But some of the disciples doubted if it was Jesus.
Jesus came to the disciples and said to them: All authority in heaven and on earth is given to me.
Do and make followers of all people in the world. Baptize them in the name of the Father and the Son and the Holy Spirit
Teach them to obey everything I have commanded you. And I will be with you always, to the end of the world.
